# Transcrição: 04-Privacy Policy & GDPR Compliance

## [00:00 - 02:43]

Hey guys, just a quick note in terms of the new GDPR regulations which also triggered updated terms of service from Facebook. So if you are working with clients within that are targeting individuals within the EU, it is so important that you make sure that your client is GDPR compliant because you roll out a funnel and it is not GDPR compliant and the client ends up getting a big fine, it's not going to look good, right? It's going to end up in litigations and stuff. So make sure that whatever funnel you're rolling out, especially if your client is targeting or potentially targeting people in the EU, is GDPR compliant. Now for the rest of the world, even if your client is a locally based client, Facebook updated their terms recently where they have stipulated that if you have a pixel on a funnel or a website, you need to make it very clear that there is a pixel and people are being tracked and what is being done with that information. So you also have to double check that your client's website has this. It's just good practice and then make sure that whatever funnels you're building going forward will also have this. Hopefully in the near future things like click funnels and lead pages and all of that jazz will have, you know, features that automatically set this up. But for now it's a manual thing. The other thing you need to make sure, I'll give you guys an example of that in a moment, but the other thing you need to make sure is that they have an updated privacy policy on their website. Feel free to go to the 8loopsocial.com website to check out our privacy policy. I will also share this link here. Make sure you edit this, but essentially they need this on every single landing page and on their website. Okay, Facebook will penalize you as well if you start running ads to a landing page that doesn't have a privacy policy. Now if we're looking at what the cookie notification might look like inside a funnel, this is kind of an example here and you can check it out through the link I supplied above, but you'll see here it's just a prominent, you know, we use cookies to help us and it clicks to the privacy policy and then continue. Basically this is just set up inside of click funnels that when people click this button it just hides this segment here. Okay, now for GDPR websites, you actually have to warn them before you can even pixel them. So if you are targeting EU, it's a whole different kettle of fish, but if you're working with a local client, just make sure you are clear. You have a privacy policy and you should be good to go.
